<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) with zero coupling confirms this is a self-contained test-only zone — all nine files are pure test consumers with no production import dependencies
- The suite covers nine distinct CLI surface areas (adapter, analyze, import, prune, quiet mode, recommend, smart-add, sync, workflow), providing broad behavioral coverage of the rex CLI contract
- Zero coupling to the rest of the codebase means these tests survive internal refactoring without needing updates to their import paths, which is the correct design for e2e tests that validate CLI boundaries not internals
- The zone correctly applies the '-tests' suffix convention: all 9 files are test files with no co-located production source, making it unambiguous that this zone contains no executable application logic.
- Coverage of smart-add and sync e2e scenarios (cli-smart-add.test.ts, cli-sync.test.ts) validates cross-package integration behaviors — these are higher-value tests than pure unit tests for catching integration regressions.
- No entry points in the e2e zone is expected and correct — test files are executed by the test runner, not imported by other modules.
